__fixture__.name: self_hosted_org_idp
__fixture__.scenario: Self-hosted boundary under self_hosted_org identity mode. The deployment integrates with a customer-run OpenID Connect IdP (via the system browser, never an embedded webview) and uses signed policy bundles. Managed convenience is absent. The visible downgrade path names self_hosted_idp_unreachable and preserves local work under the resulting local-only posture.

boundary_label: Self-hosted – customer IdP over the system browser
plain_language_summary: Aureline speaks OpenID Connect against your customer-run identity provider over the system browser. Local work is unaffected if the IdP is unreachable.

visible_downgrade_path.downgrade_note: If the self-hosted IdP is unreachable, Aureline narrows to local-only. Local work continues; provider mutations that require the IdP wait for recovery.

preserved_local_work.note: Local work is intact. Provider lanes that require the customer IdP narrow until the IdP is reachable and the system-browser sign-in completes.
retained_capabilities: Open, edit, save, and undo local workspaces.
Search over the local workspace and navigate symbols.
Run local Git commands and commit locally.
Run local tasks and debuggers in trusted workspaces.
Use BYOK AI providers and local model execution.
blocked_capabilities: Complete managed sign-in while the self-hosted IdP is unreachable.
Publish through provider lanes that require the self-hosted session.

reserved_rows.note: Passkey / WebAuthn step-up is supported via the system browser.
Reserved for the session-expiry / policy-epoch-roll / trust-downgrade reauth row.
No managed seat is held under self-hosted posture.
Declares that self-hosted deprovision MUST preserve local workspace, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI capability.
